name: azure-storage-queue-ts
description: SDK skill for Azure Queue Storage operations. Provides documentation and code examples for sending, receiving, peeking, and managing messages in Azure queues using the @azure/storage-queue package. Supports authentication with DefaultAzureCredential, connection strings, shared keys, and SAS tokens.
capabilities: Send messages to Azure Queue Storage queues
Receive and process messages from queues
Peek at messages without removing them
Update message content and visibility timeout
Delete individual messages or clear all messages
Create, list, and delete queues with metadata
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only documentation skill containing no executable code. The skill provides documentation and code examples for the @azure/storage-queue SDK. Static analysis scanned 0 files (0 lines) and detected 0 security issues. No suspicious patterns or risk factors were identified. The skill is safe for publication.
valueStatement: Integrate message queue functionality into your applications using Azure Queue Storage SDK. This skill provides ready-to-use code patterns for sending, receiving, and managing messages in Azure queues.
userTitle: Use Azure Queue Storage with TypeScript
limitations: Maximum message size is 64 KB per message
Browser support is limited (no SAS generation or shared key credential)
Requires Azure subscription and storage account

useCases.title: Build message processing workers
useCases.description: Create background workers that receive messages from Azure queues, process them, and delete them upon completion.

useCases.title: Implement task scheduling
useCases.description: Use queues to distribute tasks across multiple workers with configurable visibility timeouts for long-running operations.

useCases.title: Decouple application components
useCases.description: Enable asynchronous communication between microservices by sending and receiving messages through Azure Queue Storage.

promptTemplates.title: Send a message to Azure Queue
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to send a message to an Azure Queue Storage queue using the @azure/storage-queue SDK with DefaultAzureCredential.

promptTemplates.title: Receive and process messages
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a code example for receiving up to 10 messages from an Azure queue, processing each message, and deleting them after successful processing.

promptTemplates.title: Handle poison messages
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement poison message handling for Azure Queue Storage. Move messages that have failed processing more than 5 times to a dead-letter queue.

promptTemplates.title: Extend visibility timeout for long tasks
promptTemplates.prompt: Show how to implement visibility timeout extension for long-running message processing tasks in Azure Queue Storage.

bestPractices: Use DefaultAzureCredential instead of connection strings or account keys for better security
Always delete messages after successful processing to prevent duplicate processing
Set appropriate visibility timeout based on expected message processing time
antiPatterns: Do not hardcode storage account credentials in source code - use environment variables or managed identity
Do not forget to handle poison messages - they will block queue processing indefinitely
Do not set visibility timeout too short for long-running operations - messages will become visible again before processing completes

faq.question: What is the maximum message size in Azure Queue Storage?
faq.answer: The maximum message size is 64 KB. For larger payloads, store the data in Azure Blob Storage and send the blob URL in the queue message.

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure Queue Storage?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential for production (supports managed identity), or connection string for development. Avoid hardcoding keys.

faq.question: What happens if I do not delete a message after receiving it?
faq.answer: The message becomes visible again after the visibility timeout expires. This enables retry on failure but can cause duplicate processing if not handled.

faq.question: Can I use Azure Queue Storage in browser applications?
faq.answer: Limited support exists. Browser apps can use SAS tokens or anonymous credentials, but cannot use StorageSharedKeyCredential or generate SAS tokens.

faq.question: How do I handle failed message processing?
faq.answer: Check the dequeueCount property. Move messages that exceed a maximum retry threshold (typically 5) to a poison queue for manual review.

faq.question: What is the difference between receiveMessages and peekMessages?
faq.answer: receiveMessages removes messages temporarily (they become invisible) and provides popReceipt for deletion. peekMessages only views messages without affecting their visibility.
